feedback.text: I'm here to voice concerns over 2 major problems that have an impact on my own life. 1, there needs to be talks about killing off video games that people have paid for. This falls into a similar camp as Right to Repair and planned obsolescence. Products that people have paid for are being removed from ownership. 2. Payment processors being arbiters on what legal content is considered too spicy for them to support. Recently Steam has had to remove hundreds of games from their library due to having themes that these payment processors have deemed "inappropriate", regardless if they break the law or not. Art takes many forms. You can like it, you can hate it. But if it stokes emotions in any direction, it's art and art should be preserved.
